title: TestCase
description:
An authored Test case: input data + checks + the Callflow shapes it is
compatible with (validated, never assumed).

bindings.description:
Optional **binding pool** (the parameters axis): a walkable pool of
[`Input`] overlays with per-call `${seq}`/`${seq:N}`/`${rand:N}`
expansion, so ONE case drives a different identity per call (see
[`crate::bindings`]). Absent = single-Input behaviour, unchanged.

checkSets.description: Ids of shared [`CheckSet`]s this case pulls in.

BindingMode.description: How the pool is walked call-to-call.

oneOf.description: Sequential walk: call *n* uses entry `n % entries.len()` (wraps).

Random walk: each call picks a uniformly random entry (seeded,
deterministic per run; repeats freely).

BindingPool.description:
The authored `bindings` field of a Test case: a pool of [`Input`] overlays.
Each call resolves ONE entry (per [`BindingMode`]), merges it over the case's
base `input` (entry fields win; extras merge key-by-key), then expands the
tokens — see the module docs.

entries.description: The Input overlays (core From/To/R-URI + extras). Must be non-empty.

Check.description:
One field assertion over the anchored message. The field grammar
(ADR-0019): URI-bearing headers expose `.userInfo/.host/.port/.displayName/`
`.tag/.param(x)` (e.g. `from.userInfo`); any other header gets
present/absent/regex over its raw value (`header(X-Foo)`); the payload is
`body`; the transport endpoints are `source.ip/.port` / `dest.ip/.port`.

value.description:
Expected value: a literal, a regex (op `regex`), or a binding —
`${input.from}`, `${infra.lbVip}`.

CheckBlock.description: A bundle of [`Check`]s bound to one `<agent>.<anchor>` message selector.

on.description:
`<agent>.<anchor>`, e.g. `bob1.initialInvite`. The anchor must come from
the canonical vocabulary and be published by every compatible shape.

optional.description:
An optional block whose selector matches no recorded message is skipped;
a non-optional one **fails** (ADR-0019).

oneOf.description: `value` is a regex the extracted field must match.
`value` (after `${…}` substitution) must equal the extracted field.

CoreInput.description:
The shared input CORE a Test case supplies to a shape: From / To / R-URI
overrides (the numbers), each optional. This is both the runtime input and
the `core` of the authored JSON [`Input`](crate::model::Input) — headers /
timers join when the harness builder can honour them (no silent fields).

Input.description:
The input a Test case feeds a shape: the shared **core** (From / To / R-URI
overrides — always optional) plus per-shape **extras** (an open map until a
shape declares a typed extras schema).
